Scott Haltabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
Fair Pol-itical Practice Cornrnission
428 J street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
I an writing to request an inforrnal advice letter
concerning a possible conflict of interest of the Mayor
of the city of Pleasanton.
FACIS
Since November ]-2, 1990, Kenneth R. Mercer, the
elected l{ayor of the City of Pleasanton, has been
employed by a title 
insurance company, chicago Tit1e
Insurance, Incorporated, hereafter rtcTI .rr cTI is a
whol-ly-owned subsidiary of Allegheny International . 
The
stock of Alfegheny fnternational is traded on the New
York Stock Exchange; moreover, Allegheny International is
a Fortune 500 Conpany.
Mr. Mercer is a salaried ernployee of CTI and
receives no commissions. Although the futl range of his
duties has not been defined, in general he will be
invo]ved in marketing and pub1ic relations. 
presumably
he wi1l, attempt to convince personnel from lending or
financiaf institutions, 
and major developers, to utifize
the escrow and title 
i-nsurance services of CTI . His
prirnary responsibil ities wilf be in santa C1ara, Contra
Costa and San Mateo counties, and in the City and County
of San Francisco. He will not be assigned
responsibil ities in ALameda County, where the City of
Pleasanton is located.
CTf has offices in rnany countj-es in California but
it does not do title 
insurance or escrow business in
Alameda County. In Alameda County, title 
insurance and
escrow business involving the narne rrChicago Titlert is


Scott Hallabrin
December 24, L99O
Page 2
handled by a company caIIed chicago Title Insurance
company of Alameda county, hereafter rrchicago Al-ameda,I'
CTI does not directly or indirectly own shares
possessing more than 50 percent of the voting power of
Chicago Alamedai Chicago Alameda does not directly or
indirectly 
otr.rn shares possessing more than 50 percent of
the voting power of cTI . tloreover (a) neither entity has
a controlling ownership interest in the other, (b)
management and control is not shared between the
entities, and (c) a controll-ing owner in one is not a
controlling owner in the other. However, if cTI refers a
client to chicago Alameda for Alameda county title
insurance or escrow business, cTI receives a referral
fee, totalling 
30 percent, fron chicago Alameda.
ISSUE
Under what circurnstances will Mr. Mercerrs
employment with cTI cause him to be disqualified frorn
participating in decisions before the Pleasanton City
Counci I ?
ANALYSIS
lily analysis of this issue is set forth belorr/ for
whatever assistance it nay be.
covernment Code section 871-OOl prohibits a public
official 
from rnakinq, participating in, or using his
official 
position to influence a governmental decision in
which he knows or has reason to know he has a financiaf
interest. 
An official 
has a financial interest in a
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision witl have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on, amonq, other interests:
Any source of income, other than
gifts and other than loans by a
1A11 statutory references are to the covernment Code un]ess
otherwise indicated. 
Comrnission regulations appear at 2
Cafifornia Adninistrative code, $$18ooo, et seq. A11 references
to regulations are to Tit1e 2, Division 6 of the California
Administrative Code.


Scott Hallabrin
December 28, ].99O
Page 3
conmercial lending institution in the
regular course of busj-ness on terms
available to the public without
regard to official 
status,
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or prornised to the public
official within l-2 months prior to
the time when the decision is made.
Section 87103 (c) .
Customers of cTI are not sources of incone to I"Ir.
Mercer since he is not a 10 percent or greater owner of
the company and receives only a salary and no
commissions. However, cTr is a source of income to him
within the meaning of section 87L03(c) and because he is
an employee of cTI under sectj-on 87103(d). consequentfy,
he must disgualify hirnself from any decisj,ons which wiLl
have a reasonably foreseeable material financial affect
on cTI which is distinguishable from the decisions'
effects on the public generally. Sections 87100 and
87103.
The effect of a decision is considered rrreasonably
foreseeable'r if there is a substantial likelihood that it
wiII occur. Certainty it is not required; however, if an
effect is only a nere possibility, 
it is not rtreasonably
foreseeable.r' (Thorner Opinion, 1 FPPC Ops. L98 (No. 75-
089, Dec. 4, 1975).
In Thorner, the Commission discussed several
specific factual situations concerning the foreseeabie
effect of a governmental decision h,hen a cornpany might
receive additional business as a result of the decision.
(Thorner, supra, at pp. 205-208.) with respect to
development projects, the FppC has stated the following
circumstances would cause a city Council's decision on a
proposed development to foreseeably generate business for
a title 
company:
rr ( 1) At the tirne of the
decj-sion, the title 
insurance company
has entered into an agreenent with
the developer to handle the title
rnsurance or escroh, business for the
proposed development;




scott HalLabrin
December 28, l99O
Page 4
(2) At the time of the
decision, the title 
insurance company
is negotiating an agreement with the
developer to handte the title
insurance or escror", business for the
proposed development; or
(3) At the tine of the
decision, the title 
insurance company
ordinarily handles the title
insurance or escrow business for the
developerr s pro j ects. tl
(otHara Advice Letter,
No. f-87-44, pages 3-4. )
Because cTI does not do title 
or escrow business in
Alameda county, the circurnstances under (1) or (2) above
would appear inapplicable. However, if cTI ordinarily
handles the titl-e insurance and/or escrov, busi,ness for a
developer, and if that developer has a project before the
Pleasanton City Council, then the provisions of (3) would
appear to be applicable. In that circumstance, Mr.
Mercer hrould be disqualified from participating in the
decision if the effect on CTI would be naterial, as
discussed below.
Because of the similarity in the names of CTI and
chicago A1ameda, and because at the tirne of a Council
decision Chicago Alameda could have an agreement with the
applicant to handle the title 
insurance or escrow
business for the proposed developrnent, would that fact
foreseeably generate business for cTi triggering his
disqualification?
Section 18706 of the Regulations provides that an
official 
has a financial interest in a decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision wiII have a
material financial effect on a business entity which is a
parent or subsidiary of, or is otherwise related to, a
business entity in which the official 
has a financial
interest.
. 
A parent-subsidiary exists $/hen one corporation
directly or indirectly owns shares possessin! *o." ihun
50 percent of the voting power of another coiporation.
Sectj.on 18236(a) of the Regulations. CTI has no parent_




Scott HaIlabrin
December 2a, L99O
Page 5
subsidiary relationship with chicago Al-ameda based on the
above-mentioned test.
Business entities which do not have a parent-
subsidiary relationship are Itotherwise relatedtt if any
one of the following three tests j-s rnet:
(1) one business entity has a controlling
ownership interest in the other business
entity .
(2) There is shared managernent and
control betvreen the entities. 
In deterrnining
whether there is shared management and control ,
consideration should be given to the following
factors:
(A) The sane person or
substantially the same person ohrns
and manages the two entities;
(B) There are common or
commingled funds or assets i
(c) The business entities share
the use of the sarne offices or
employees, or otherwise share
activities, 
resources or personnel on
a regular basis;
(D) There is otherwj,se a
regular and close working
relationship between the entitiesi or
(3) A control-ling or4rner (50 percent or
greater interest as a sharehoLder or as a
general partner) in one entity also is a
controlling owner in the other entity.
Section 18236 (b) of
the Regulations .
Based on these tests, CTI is not otherwise related to
Chicago Afameda.
Notwithstanding that CTI is not related to Chicago
Alameda, there is one situation involving CTI and Chi6ago




Scott Hallabrin
December 2A, 1990
Page 5
Al-ameda which would reasonabl,y generate business for cTI .
If cTI refers a c1i-ent to chicago Alameda for Alameda
County title 
insurance or escrow business, cTI receives a
referral fee, totalling 30 percent, from chicago Alameda.
Should this referred cl,ient be involved in a matter
before the city Council, the foreseeable portion of the
test would be met and Mr. Mercer would be disqualified
frorn participating in the decision if the effect on CTI
would be material-.
Regulation fa702.2 contains guidelines for
deterrnining naterialitv. 
These guidelines vary with the
financial size of the business entity. 
Because AIlegheny
International, which owns CTI is traded on the New York
Stock Exchange, and is a Fortune 5OO Company, the effect
of the decision will be material if the decision (L) v/iII
result in an increase or decrease to the company t s gross
revenues for a fiscal year of $1-,000,000 or morei or (2)
wil-1 result in the companyrs incurring or avoiding
additional expenses or reducing or elirninating existing
expenses for a fiscal year in an amount of $25o,0o0 or
morei or (3) will result in the companyrs increase or
decrease in the value of assets or liabilities 
of
$l-,0O0,000 or more. See S1,8702.2 (a) (1) .
CONCII'SION
Due to CTIrs lack of business in Alaneda County and
because the threshold arnounts are extremely high, a
conflict of interest should not ordinarily arise in
context of Mr. Mercerrs decisions as the Mayor of
Pleasanton. In most cases, consequently, I conclude that
Mr. Mercerrs enrployment with CTI \,ril-l not creale a
conflict of interest vis-a-vis his seat on the Cj-ty
council.
I woul-d appreciate your conments on this analysis
and your specific advj-ce as to when !lr. Mercerrs
ernplo)4nent with CTI will result in Mr. Mercerrs being
disqualified from participating in decisions before €he
Pleasanton City Councit.


Scott Hallabrin
December 28, 1990
Page 7
If you have any questions or need further
information, please feel free to contact me.
very truly yours, 
/)
?hil,a/t&*<
I'{ICHAEL H. ROUSH
city Attorney

cc: 
Kenneth R. Mercer, I"layor

sections.question_synthetic: The City of Pleasanton's city attorney seeks FPPC advice on when Mayor Kenneth R. Mercer's employment with Chicago Title Insurance Company (CTI) would create a conflict of interest requiring his disqualification from city council decisions.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city attorney to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document includes a detailed ANALYSIS section containing the requester's legal analysis and interpretation of relevant law, but this is not an FPPC conclusion. The letter explicitly requests FPPC comments and advice. Document dated December 28, 1990; document ID appears to be UNK-91-10261 indicating FPPC's internal filing.

embedding.summary: City of Pleasanton city attorney Michael H. Roush requests an informal FPPC advice letter regarding potential conflicts of interest arising from Mayor Kenneth R. Mercer's employment with Chicago Title Insurance Company, and provides a detailed analysis of applicable conflict of interest provisions and regulations.
